reviews.review_description:
Vader Immortal is not a game. It's an interactive movie. 

Mobility is extremely hampered and you are frozen in place while you keep pushing the joystick, trying to move while you watch the movie. 

The episodes are very short and you have to pay for them one at a time for $10 each. I could not find any way to order all three at the same time. 
Even the three episodes together do not make a full game or movie. 

I did not like the robot sidekick, played by Maya Rudolph. Right off the bat it said, "my bad" and then later it says, "I'm not gonna lie". They do not use slang from the viewers time in Star Wars. It ruins the story. 
 
The VR and animation is very good and it's the best part. And the sound is also very good. It's amazing really. So far the story is just okay. I have only watched Episode 1 and 2.

In the end it's a good experience but it should be half the price. 

It made me want to play a real Star Wars game, and don't don't say Tales from Galaxy's Edge. I mean a real Star Wars game. But Galaxy's Edge is a better game than Vader Immortal .

reviews.review_title: A Short but Brilliant VR Star Wars Exper
reviews.review_description:
I played through all three episodes of the Vader Immortal trilogy, and honestly, I really enjoyed it. Yes, each part is pretty short on its own, but experiencing them together as one complete story feels very satisfying. I took my time exploring the environments and ended up spending about two hours across all three episodes. The locations are gorgeous, and the atmosphere pulls you right in.
Just a heads-up: this isn’t an action-heavy game. It’s more of an interactive cinematic experience, but it’s executed really well, with some genuinely epic moments. The lightsaber mechanics are a real highlight - training, swinging the blade, and deflecting blaster bolts feel incredibly satisfying and immersive in VR, even though the combat stays simple and accessible.
I’m not a hardcore Star Wars fan these days, but I grew up watching the films and loved them. Getting to step back into that universe in VR after all these years was a real treat. Standing face-to-face with Vader actually gave me chills.
At full price, the length might feel a bit brief for some, but on sale, the complete trilogy is an absolute steal. If you’re looking for a polished, atmospheric VR adventure that lets you live out a few iconic Star Wars moments, this is absolutely worth picking up.

A good Star Wars game for beginners I wish it was longer gameplay When the storm trooper exits, the elevator in the guys fight you can’t hit them You can’t fire guns in space it’s not really a puzzle game. It’s more of a easy to play 
game. The price is a little high. It should be a free trial type of game so you can see if you want to buy it. ￼￼ But I’m a Star Wars fan, so the only thing that kept me going was the Star Wars Factor ￼ of the game You can only go back into your ship to touch certain stuff. I wish you could touch it all but that might take a lot of coding overall it’s an OK game. I do like the light saber Fights.￼

It's more of a cinematic experience than a traditional game. The graphics are impressive, especially considering how old the game is. You do interact, but everything is scripted—there's no risk of dying, and it doesn't offer much of a challenge, though it's still fun to play. Each episode ties into the same story and lasts about 30 minutes. For such a short and old game, paying 10 dollars feels a bit steep, but if you pick it up on sale, it's definitely worth it. I bought all three episodes for 10 dollars, which I think is a fair price.

Terrible voice acting, incredibly annoying and took me out of the moment, the droid in particular. Game was way too short, if I had known it was so short I would never have bought it. Not worth the $10 go buy some ice cream instead.

reviews.review_title: Pretty good game but too short
reviews.review_description:
The game is really good and I had a lot of fun but there’s only one fight scene you know when they took me days to complete it cause I’m lazy to play the game and I usually play different games anyways back to what I was saying the game was like maybe 25 minutes long, which even an hour for people who are dumb anyways you should download this game if you’re OK with it being short
￼ And if you’re a big fan of Star Wars like me ￼

I was so hyped in the middle of the game, and how tuff Darth Vader is, but then it suddenly ends! Like bro, you should combine all three episodes together instead of have you pay $10 for each episode when you beat the game in one sitting. My point is, MAKE THE DANG GAME LONGER!!!!!! But, the game was awesome other than how short it is.

All 3 episodes of this game are worth it for the Lightsaber Dojo alone……….advancing thru 40 levels or so in each game!!
Outstanding, and the higher levels can be quite fast-paced, a bit of a workout!!  The game/story portion is great too (albeit short, as others report), but the training dojo’s offer countless hours of fun honing your skills!!!

It's an awesome Star Wars VR game, especially if you are a fan, then you would love it. HOWEVER, the most disappointing part is that all episodes are way too short. I'd say each episode is around 20 minutes. Developers could've just combined them together. Also, not a lot of walking... you will stay in one position more than roaming around. Nonetheless, graphics, storyline, and gameplay are pretty decent.
Why do you have to teleport to move, like it's so slow and it's short for the price, it barely has anything to do.

reviews.review_title: Very short game for the money.
reviews.review_description: My son was disappointed. He used some birthday money and was expecting more from this. It only took him 10 minutes.
So cool, feel like I was there. When Darth Vader showed up was my favorite part. Make a clone wars one, please!
Just long enough to not get a refund

First, i agree with other reviews that it plays less like s game and is more like an interactive movie.

but most importantly, this was mathe 15 minutes of play/time, if that. They really should say this before people pay $10 for it. I was expecting a more substantial amount of entertainment. It feels like a scam, ending so quickly. Seriously. I cannot recommend this purchase.

reviews.review_title: A must have trilogy for Star Wars fans!!
reviews.review_description:
I bought the trilogy bundle while it was on sale, despite a lot of reviews saying it wasn't working. I have only played the first episode so far but I can confirm it IS working now and I can also confirm two other things.

1. Yes, the episodes story modes are quite short, but, if you're a Star Wars fan you will absolutely love these titles despite their shortness.

2  The very first thing I saw was an option saying LIGHTSABER DOJO. I walked up to the lightsaber, picked it up and switched it on and couldn't help but say out loud "HOLY S**T THIS IS SO COOL!!!" So I say again, if you're a Star Wars fan, you MUST experience Vader Immortal!

reviews.review_title: Paid $10 for all 3. Was still a rip off.
reviews.review_description: These are NOT games, these are VR movies with decent graphics and *barely* interactive segments.  You can only move in a predefined path and only perform a few limited actions that, once completed, cue the story to move forward.  There is a reason the total purchases of each installment drops off by several thousand (currently 1st-16K, 2nd-7.4K, 3rd-5.5K).  All 3 start to finish was less than 2 hours of playtime, but you can't return them once you've finished even one chapter.  What a joke.

Great game I played all 3 and they were good the only things I hate are.

1. The games are to short 


2. The lightsabers you get in the dojo are not playable in the story


3. the cliffhanger at the end of game 3 


But all in all its a good game and I bought them on a discount so they were worth the 4$ I payed for then so... yeah Great games.

** MY OPINION (complete trilogy): A HUGE FAN FAVORITE! ** 
EPIC, GRANDIOSE, MASTERFUL… AND ABOVE ALL, INCREDIBLY IMMERSIVE!
Visually stunning, Vader Immortal impresses with its lighting effects, iconic environments, legendary sound effects (the lightsaber), and a soundtrack worthy of the films.
It's incredibly easy to pick up and play, fully customizable, and motion sickness-free: a perfect entry point into VR, even for beginners.
🔴 Episode I: brilliant setup, the first encounter with Darth Vader is impressive!
🔴 Episode II: shorter but packed with revelations and iconic scenes!
🔴 Episode III: intense and memorable finale, a truly apotheosis!
SHORT BUT INTENSE
Each episode is relatively short (45–60 minutes if you take your time), but the DOJOS change everything. They offer tremendous replayability! Up to 3 hours per dojo, with progressively increasing difficulty, and combined use of the lightsaber AND the Force in the final episode.
😈 MEET DARTH VADER IN VR!
Seeing him, fighting alongside him, then facing him… it's an emotional rollercoaster and an experience every Star Wars fan simply must have. This trilogy alone justifies buying a Quest for any fan of the saga. A childhood dream come true.
THE PROS - THE CONS
Total Star Wars immersion. Excellent VR comfort. Accessible to beginners. Ultra-fun and long-lasting dojos. Darth Vader… simply amazing! But the main adventure is rather short. We always want more 😉
🎯 CONCLUSION: AN ABSOLUTE HIT!
Vader Immortal – The Trilogy is an unmissable VR experience, to be played in one sitting. A narrative gem, spectacular and emotional, designed for both fans and newcomers to VR.

I really like it the lightsaber and force mechanics are really cool to play with but the story is too short for it to be $10 per game. Especially for littler people who can’t afford it. You can’t cliff hanger someone who’s not gonna be able to get the rest of the story. It should be at least $2 or free. Good storyline, good graphics, good acting, good mechanics, bad price.

Finished in an hour!
So don’t think you can buy one game, you’ll need the set.

This making it not value for money.

They should also have other games with in them

I love these Vader immortal games, bought all 3 and completed the story. After completing them I realised something. The first one didn’t have the force because it wasn’t introduced until episode 2. This made it worse but better. Episode 2 and 3 continued and finished the story but their dojo was the same. Force and all, however this made it worse. It felt too easy because using the force was super overpowered and took away the challenging aspect but the first one had more of a technical impact making me feel it was my saber skill and not the force carrying me.thats why I refunded 2 and 3 because they were pretty much the same and 1 was unique in a way. Hope this helps but I do reccomend to buy all 3, complete the story then refund 2 and 3 and enjoy the 1st ones dojo which is why I give 4 stars only because of the weird lightsaber twitch thing that has a simple and small fix

reviews.review_title: Great experiences, not so much a game
reviews.review_description:
All three episodes are worth it if you know what youre getting into and know these are more of an interactive experience than a game, but there is the training dojo at the end that is replayable and honestly all of those are pretty fun...

My review scale*
5- Amazing game, while still might not be perfect any flaws are forgivable, is well worth your time
4- Good game, might have a few minor issues or missed opportunities keeping it from being better, but is very playable and still an enjoyable experience
3- Mid tier game, not great but not awful, these games can go either way depending on who you are or what you want or expect out of the game
2- Not a very good game, but not completely broken or unplayable, you might really need to want to play this game to get any enjoyment out of it
1- Awful game, broken or unplayable, I don't see these games being for anyone unless significant updates fix the game

*This is an overly simplified review scale since meta does not allow for half stars so in some cases I may round a game up or down depending on a case by case scenario, 
my reviews are always honest and in no way swayed by anybody else and regardless if I received a game for free or not, these ratings and reviews are however part of a 
campaign for us as consumers to be better about reviewing games and helping bring the games we love the attention they deserve.
I loved the game but it’s only about 30 minutes of gameplay do not recommend also that’s about 33 cents a minute

If you want to go into light speed and fight with and against Darth Vader these three games are for you. I am not the biggest of star wars fans but I loved playing these games.

Ok look i got this game when i first got my quest and it was amazing. But like i said this game is amazing you feel like a real jedi!!!!!!!!!  And i would pay another 30 for 3 more episodes  like. I’m ￼￼old enough to have seen the original Star Wars movie in the ￼theater and haven’t played video games in decades so I was ￼hesitant to try an older game like Vader Immortal but I must admit it’s incredible and not too hard for an old fool like myself.  I only wish I could travel back in time and play it as a ten year old.

Update:

With the version 83 OS release Meta has fixed whatever they broke in their previous OS  release and Vader Immortal is working on Quest 3 now.

While I'm glad I can play this VR classic again, it's disconcerting that Meta and / or game developers can take away games you've purchased at any time with buggy software updates.

Note that the developer responses to bug reports are worthless. If you send an email to the address given you get a reply back saying that they don't support the Quest version and to contact Meta.

So no one maintains Vader Immortal's code.

It's abandonware at the mercy of Meta's ever changing buggy OS.

Previous Review:

The recent Meta OS update has broken the game. It now just sits forever at the loading screen.

Hopefully Meta will fix their glitchy update, (likely introducing new glitches in the process), and this will be playable again but until then it's dead in the water.

developer_response.body: Please reach out to our support team at https://support.starwars.com or support@starwars.com to see if we can help.

reviews.review_description: This game is an amazing experience for VR. The graphics are fair, the interaction and free movement is really limited. The game is really short, and under 2 hrs. The lightsaber fights are really fun and will work up a sweat. But im glad I only paid $5 each episode, because it’s not really worth paying full price for all 3. The only let down is how short the games are. I really feel like it’s a demo more than anything, but it’s good for a replay or have friends or family try it out when company comes over.

Everything was amazing, and there are only two bad things. The walking is a bit disappointing because you can’t actually walk; it’s just teleporting. The second thing is that it’s too short. After only about an hour—maybe two—I beat it, and I bought all three games. Overall, it’s a fantastic game. I think if you’re into action, you should buy it. However, I am on Quest 2, so if you’re not on Quest 2, you shouldn’t take my word for it.

Even though this is the very first series of Star Wars VR games, the game was professionally developed as it harbours great quality in many aspects. The overall quality is very high, and it never disappoints Star Wars fans.


The graphics are highly detailed. The sound and music are epic, as all Star Wars media should be. The atmosphere, the motion, the special effects, the environment, the scene transitions, the feeling—everything looks great. They truly draw out all the essences of Star Wars and put them together in this VR experience. This is what a Star Wars VR game is meant to be.


Although the story length in each episode is quite short, it is very enjoyable. In Episode 1, it sets the background, provides learning and lightsabre training, but it also does not forget to place the player in intense combat situations.


Amongst all three games in the series, the Lightsabre Dojo mode in this Episode 1 is the best gameplay for fitness purposes. Due to the lack of Force abilities, the player is compelled to rely solely on his sabre skills and perception in both sword fighting and projectile defence, which results in a more intense workout.

Controls are no longer being recognized for grabbing the lightsaber after the latest update on Meta Quest 3.

EDIT:  Was fixed!

Rating: ★★★☆☆ (3/5)

Vader Immortal on Meta Quest promises an epic journey into the Star Wars universe, and in many ways, it delivers—cinematic storytelling, intense lightsaber duels, and a palpable sense of presence alongside Darth Vader himself. But when it comes to gameplay mechanics—especially climbing—there’s a noticeable disconnect between what’s shown in the trailers and what you actually get.

In the trailers, climbing looks dynamic and immersive. You imagine yourself scrambling up walls in dark Sith temples or hanging over bottomless pits like a galactic Indiana Jones. But in practice? Climbing is mostly an illusion. Instead of physically pulling yourself up or scaling walls with intuitive motion, the game often just teleports you to predetermined positions after minimal interaction. It breaks immersion and feels more like a scripted cutscene than an actual part of the gameplay.

This isn’t a deal-breaker for everyone, especially since the rest of the experience—the visuals, atmosphere, and lightsaber combat—is still impressive on the Meta Quest. The headset handles the environments beautifully, and the motion controls shine during combat. But for players expecting full VR climbing interactivity based on the marketing, it’s a letdown.

Pros:

Incredible Star Wars atmosphere with high production values

Lightsaber combat is smooth and satisfying

Great voice acting, especially Vader

Works well on Meta Quest with solid performance


Cons:

Climbing mechanics feel underdeveloped and overly scripted

Misleading trailer footage suggests more interaction than delivered

Short overall experience with limited exploration
There's just too much talking and not enough fighting. It's a good game but it's short and the Force is kind of goofy still a pretty good game but not enough fighting. Same thing with episode 2
amazing game if I had to live in one vr game it would definitely be this game but  they could have a little more fighting but the graphics are really good
